audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim final tribal blessing of Moses, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Sinai/Seir/Paran theophany, Torah inheritance, Jeshurun kingship, tribal blessings from Reuben through Asher, Levi's vocation, Joseph abundance, warrior imagery, tribal omissions, and final praise of Israel's help and refuge.

verses.restored: This is blessing with which Moses, man of Divine, blessed children of Israel before his death.
He said: YHWH came from Sinai and dawned from Seir upon them; he shone from Mount Paran and came from holy myriads; from his right hand was fiery law/fire of decree for them.
Indeed he loves peoples/tribes; all his holy ones are in your hand. They sat down at your feet; each receives from your words.
Moses commanded us Torah/instruction, an inheritance for assembly of Jacob.
He was king in Jeshurun when heads of people assembled, together tribes of Israel.
May Reuben live and not die, though his men be few/not few.
And this for Judah. He said: Hear, YHWH, voice of Judah and bring him to his people. His hands contend for him; be help against his adversaries.
Of Levi he said: Your Thummim and your Urim belong to your faithful one, whom you tested at Massah, with whom you contended at waters of Meribah,
who said of his father and mother, I have not seen them, and did not recognize his brothers, and did not know his children; for they kept your word and guarded your covenant.
They shall teach your judgments to Jacob and your Torah to Israel; they shall put incense before your nose and whole burnt offering upon your altar.
Bless, YHWH, his strength/resources, and accept work of his hands. Shatter loins of those rising against him and those hating him, so they rise no more.
Of Benjamin he said: Beloved of YHWH, he dwells securely beside him; he shields him all day, and between his shoulders he dwells.
Of Joseph he said: Blessed by YHWH is his land, with precious gifts of heavens, with dew, and with deep lying beneath,
with precious produce of sun and precious yield of moons,
with finest from ancient mountains and precious things of age-long hills,
with precious things of earth and its fullness, and favor of the one dwelling in bush. May it come upon head of Joseph and crown of one set apart from his brothers.
Firstborn of his bull—majesty is his; horns of wild ox are his horns. With them he gores peoples together to ends of earth. They are myriads of Ephraim, and they are thousands of Manasseh.
Of Zebulun he said: Rejoice, Zebulun, in your going out, and Issachar, in your tents.
Peoples they shall call to mountain; there they shall sacrifice sacrifices of righteousness, for abundance of seas they shall suck and hidden treasures of sand.
Of Gad he said: Blessed is one enlarging Gad. Like lioness he dwells and tears arm, even crown of head.
He saw first portion for himself, for there a ruler's portion was reserved; he came with heads of people. Justice of YHWH he did and his judgments with Israel.
Of Dan he said: Dan is lion cub leaping from Bashan.
Of Naphtali he said: Naphtali, satisfied with favor and full of blessing of YHWH, possess west/sea and south.
Of Asher he said: Blessed above sons is Asher; may he be favored by his brothers and dip his foot in oil.
Iron and bronze are your bars/sandals, and as your days, so your strength/rest.
There is none like Divine of Jeshurun, riding heavens as your help and clouds in his majesty.
The eternal Divine is refuge, and underneath are everlasting arms. He drove enemy from before you and said, Destroy.
Israel dwells securely, alone; spring of Jacob in land of grain and new wine, and his heavens drop dew.
Happy are you, Israel! Who is like you, a people saved by YHWH, shield of your help and sword of your majesty? Your enemies shall cringe before you, and you shall tread upon their high places.

translation_notes: Verse 2 contains difficult and ancient wording, especially eshdat/fiery law, and requires textual-witness audit rather than overconfident harmonization.
Verse 3 may refer to peoples, tribes, or holy ones; its syntax is compressed and uncertain.
Verse 6 has an ambiguity concerning whether Reuben's men are few or not few.
Simeon receives no separate blessing in this chapter, a significant tribal omission requiring literary and historical audit.
Levi's blessing praises radical covenant allegiance and teaching vocation, but verse 9 must not be used to demand family abandonment or clerical cruelty.
Joseph's blessing celebrates ecological abundance—dew, deep waters, sun, seasons, mountains, earth, and fullness.
Wild-ox, lion, goring, shattering, sword, and enemy-destruction images are ancient tribal-war rhetoric and must not authorize modern violence or ethnic domination.
The final refuge image—everlasting arms beneath Israel—must not be superseded or reassigned away from Jewish covenant continuity.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 33 to justify tribal supremacy, family abandonment, clerical violence, conquest, ethnic domination, or supersessionism. Receive the blessing as a diverse, textually difficult tribal poem whose strongest enduring images are Torah as inheritance, land abundance, secure dwelling, Divine refuge, and everlasting arms beneath the people.
